title: Palindrome Check
description:
Given a string S consisting of lowercase English letters, determine whether it is a palindrome.

A string is a palindrome if it reads the same forward and backward.

Input:
The first line contains a single string S.

Output:
Print "YES" if the string is a palindrome, otherwise print "NO".
constraints:
1 <= |S| <= 10^5
S consists of lowercase English letters only

boilerplateCode.python:
s = input()
# Check if s is a palindrome
# Print "YES" or "NO"

title: Count Frequency
description:
Given an array of N integers, count the frequency of each distinct element and print them in the order of their first appearance.

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N — the number of elements.
The second line contains N space-separated integers.

Output:
For each distinct element (in order of first appearance), print the element and its frequency separated by a space, one per line.

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Count frequency of each element
# Print element and frequency in order of first appearance

title: FizzBuzz Classic
description:
Given an integer N, print all numbers from 1 to N. But for multiples of 3, print "Fizz" instead of the number. For multiples of 5, print "Buzz". For multiples of both 3 and 5, print "FizzBuzz".

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N.

Output:
Print N lines: for each number from 1 to N, print the appropriate output.

testCases.expectedOutput:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
# Print FizzBuzz from 1 to n

title: Matrix Transpose
description:
Given an R x C matrix of integers, print its transpose.

The transpose of a matrix is obtained by converting rows to columns and columns to rows.

The next R lines each contain C space-separated integers.

Output:
Print the transposed matrix: C lines, each containing R space-separated integers.

title: Merge Sorted Arrays
description:
Given two sorted arrays of sizes N and M respectively, merge them into a single sorted array.

The third line contains M space-separated integers (sorted in ascending order).

title: Balanced Parentheses
description:
Given a string S containing only the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string has balanced parentheses.

A string is balanced if:
- Every opening bracket has a corresponding closing bracket of the same type.
- Brackets are closed in the correct order.

Input:
The first line contains a single string S.

Output:
Print "YES" if the string has balanced parentheses, otherwise print "NO".

title: Next Greater Element
description:

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description:
Given an array of N integers, find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence (LIS).

A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from the array by deleting some or no elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N — the number of elements.
The second line contains N space-separated integers.

Output:
Print a single integer — the length of the longest increasing subsequence.

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Find length of longest increasing subsequence

title: Graph BFS Shortest Path
description:
Given an unweighted undirected graph with N nodes (numbered 1 to N) and M edges, find the shortest path distance from node 1 to node N. If no path exists, print -1.

Each of the next M lines contains two integers U and V — representing an edge between node U and node V.
